question_text: A solar panel system costs £5000 to install and produces energy worth £400 per year. An equivalent diesel generator costs £2000 to install but requires £600 of fuel per year. Compare the two options over a 10-year period and recommend which is more cost-effective for a remote location that must be self-sufficient for energy.
correct_answer: Solar: £5000 + (£0 × 10) = £5000 total. Diesel: £2000 + (£600 × 10) = £8000 total. Solar is more cost-effective over 10 years by £3000. For a remote location, solar is also better because it's renewable and doesn't require fuel supply chains.
explanation: Students must calculate total costs over time and consider that renewable energy has no fuel costs, making it cheaper long-term despite higher initial investment.
